channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: LEC EWC Qualifier Recap: G2 and KC Advance While Galions Upset Again
item.content:encoded:
The League of Legends EMEA Esports World Cup Qualifiers continued this week and led to some pretty interesting matches.

Both tier-two ERL teams were still in the tournament and looked to upset the status quo and the qualifiers have become even more important for the French LFL teams with the Esports World Cup moving to Paris.

And the Upper Bracket concluded with G2 Esports, Natus Vincere, Karmine Corp, and Movistar KOI looking to secure their place in the first qualifier match.

Here’s how the latest round of the EMEA LoL Esports World Cup Qualifiers played out.

Galions Keep EWC Dream Alive

The ERL teams continued to prove their quality in the Lower Bracket against LEC opposition.

EMEA Masters Champions Solary had impressed in the previous round after sweeping Fnatic.

Yet, they couldn’t get past GIANTX, despite the best efforts of star jungler Lanzo ‘Zicssi’ Ciajolo.

Twice, Zicssi went in alone with Pantheon ult and stole an objective, both Baron and Elder Drake, but it wasn’t enough for Solary to come back versus GIANTX.

Meanwhile, fellow LFL team Galions stomped Shifters 2-0 to continue progressing after sweeping SK Gaming 2-0 in the previous round.

ADC Franciszek HARPOON’ Gryszkiewicz had a particularly great series, finishing the first match 10/2/2 as Lucian.

Now Galions will look to make it three upsets in a row when they face NAVI next.

G2 and Karmine Corp Advance To First Qualifier Match

In the Upper Bracket, G2 Esports and Karmine Corp advanced to the first EWC qualifier after clean sweeps.

G2 dominated Natus Vincere, defeating them 2-0 to remain undefeated in the qualifiers.

NAVI had a good early start to the second game, with Yoon ‘Poby’ Sung-won solokilling Rasmus ‘Caps’ Winther in lane.

However, G2 then quickly fought back, and NAVI couldn’t find an answer to their intelligent teamfighting.

Meanwhile, Movistar KOI and KCorp had a rematch from their heated series during the LEC Madrid Roadtrip just a few days prior.

But this time it didn’t go to all three matches as KCorp took the clean 2-0 victory.

Much has been said about the impressive form of MKOI’s midlaner Joseph Joon ‘Jojopyun’ Pyun while KCorp’s mid Kang ‘kyeahoo’ Ye-hoo has been labelled as a possible weakpoint for the team.

However, Kyeahoo got the better of this match-up this time with a huge 8/0/7 game on Anivia in the second game.

KCorp ADC Caliste ‘Caliste’ Henry-Hennebert almost got his second penta against MKOI this week but had to settle for a quadra to close out the series.

G2 and KCorp will now play each other in a best-of-five to decide the first EMEA team to qualify for the Esports World Cup.

Can KCorp get revenge and do G2 just have their number? Share your predictions with us on the Insider Gaming Discord.

Meanwhile in the ERLs, NLC has released a statement apologising after frustrating the league’s former casters.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Hell Let Loose: Vietnam is Launching in June, With Its Beta Starting in Just Two Weeks
item.content:encoded:
Team17 and Hell Let Loose: Vietnam developer Expression Games have announced that the game will launch in June, with its open Beta starting in just two weeks.

The announcement revealed that Hell Let Loose: Vietnam is set to launch on June 18 for the PlayStation 5, Xbox Series S|X, and PC. Physical versions of the game will launch later in the summer on August 4 for the PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series S|X.

In addition to the game’s release date announcement, Espression Games has also revealed that the game is set to have an open beta later this month, starting on May 29. The open beta (free to everyone) will only be available on PC.

You can watch the brand-new trailer below:

The official game description reads, “Fight across large-scale jungle battlefields inspired by real-world locations during one of history’s fiercest conflicts. Coordinate with your squad in brutal 50 vs 50 warfare, pilot helicopters, command patrol boats and battle through dense terrain where teamwork is everything.”

It continues, “Players can also join the free Open Beta Weekend on Steam from May 29 through June 1 for a chance to experience Hell Let Loose: Vietnam before launch.”

I managed to get hands-on with Hell Let Loose: Vietnam at Gamescom last year, where I described the game as “familiar, but entirely different”. The game feels like the Hell Let Loose we all know and love, but its atmosphere makes it feel entirely different.

Will you be playing Hell Let Loose: Vietnam on June 18 and the open beta later in May? You can take the discussion even further in the official Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, read about Take-Two Interactive adding nearly $2 billion in value amid Grand Theft Auto 6 pre-order rumors. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Tom Henderson is Insider Gaming's Co-Founder and Editor-In-Chief. When he's not running one of the industry's leading independent video game websites, he's probably playing an FPS like Call of Duty,…

More from Tom Henderson

item.title: Today’s Wordle #1791: Hints and Answer for May 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Wordle challenges players to guess a fresh five-letter word each day in six tries or fewer. Whether you’re a beginner or a daily player, today’s tips and strategies can help you solve it faster and maintain your streak—if you’re open to a little extra help. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Wordle, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answer for Wordle Game #1791 for May 15, 2026

To play today’s Wordle, head to the game’s website. If you want to go right to today’s answer without any tips or tricks, you can click right here.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s and yesterday’s editions of Wordle. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Wordle

Wordle is a simple but addictive word puzzle. Each day, players have to guess a five-letter word answer in no more than six attempts. After each guess:

  • Green letters are correct and in the right position.
  • Yellow letters are correct, but in the wrong position.
  • Gray letters are not in the word at all.

The goal is to guess the word in as few tries as possible. Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Wordle can only be played on the New York Times website. There is currently no app for you to download the official game.

Wordle Hints For Today, May 15, 2026

Today’s Wordle answer for game #1791 is another challenging puzzle. According to the NY Times Wordle review, today’s answer takes an average of 5.3 out of 6 guesses. Despite it being tougher, it’s still a full guess easier than the 6.3-guess average from May 14.

General Wordle Hints For May 15

Without giving today’s Wordle answer completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: It refers to a set of beliefs or principles held by a person or group.
  • Hint 2: It’s often associated with religion, ideology, or guiding values.
  • Hint 3: The word can describe something officially stated or deeply held.
  • Hint 4: It may be recited, written, or followed as a foundation for behavior.
  • Hint 5: Wants to be taken higher

Use these clues to narrow down your guesses and increase your chances of solving Wordle #1791 for May 15, 2026.

Starter Words For May 15, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Wordle answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you figure out some included letters. A few starter words that may help are:

  • RADIO
  • BRINE
  • CLAMP
  • CROWN

Today’s Wordle Answer For Game #1791 On May 15, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answer to today’s Wordle below.

Answer: CREED

If you solved today’s Wordle without our answer, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

What Does Today’s Wordle Answer Mean?

CREED is a noun that means “any system, doctrine, or formula of religious belief, as of a denomination.”

Yesterday’s Wordle Answer

If you missed the previous game, here’s yesterday’s Wordle answer for Game #1790 on May 13, 2026

Answer: WAVER

While not always the case, looking at previous words can sometimes give you insight into common patterns or letter usage in Wordle puzzles.

When Is The Next Wordle Game After #1791

Wordle games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours before to give players a heads up. The game on May 16 will be Wordle Game #1792.

What Is Wordle?

Wordle is a daily word puzzle created by Josh Wardle. Initially developed as a prototype in 2013, it wasn’t fully released until 2021. The game challenges players to guess a five-letter word in six tries or fewer, using hints from each attempt to narrow down the answer. Its straightforward concept, accessibility, and the thrill of solving a new puzzle each day helped it surge in popularity by late 2021. The success of Wordle inspired numerous spin-offs and social media challenges, leading to its acquisition by The New York Times in early 2022.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw
